title: Aureline flaky-history and quarantine record
description: Boundary schema for the per-test-item flake-aggregation and quarantine record family every test surface (test runner UI, test tree panel, AI tool-call mediator, support exporter, replay / import probe, history row) reads when it needs to answer 'is this test currently flaky, what is its recent pass / fail history, is it currently quarantined, who quarantined it, why, and when does the quarantine expire'. Flaky history aggregates outcomes across attempts of the same canonical test item; quarantine is the typed authority record that pins a test under flake / known-failing / environment-dependent / under-investigation policy. The two records share one schema so a UI / CLI / support reviewer reads the same vocabulary across both. The schema cites - and never re-mints - the canonical test item id from schemas/execution/test_discovery_state.schema.json#test_item_record, the per-attempt outcome events from schemas/execution/run.schema.json, the command-dispatch descriptor (ADR-0016), the admin-policy epoch (workspace policy pack), and the cancellation-authority vocabulary on schemas/execution/attempt.schema.json. Raw command lines, raw stdout / stderr byte streams, raw env bodies, raw API request / response bodies, raw absolute paths, raw URLs, raw secret values, raw test names, raw assertion bodies, and raw stack traces MUST NOT cross any of these boundaries; records carry refs, hashes, counts, and class labels only. Adding a new enum member, a new optional field, or a new sub-record kind is additive-minor with a flaky_history_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing member is breaking and requires a new decision row.

flaky_classification_class.description: Closed flaky-classification vocabulary. not_flaky_stable_history is the typed value for a test whose recent history shows no intermittency above the threshold; suspected_flaky_recent_intermittent is the typed honest mid-state when a small sample has shown intermittency but the threshold is not met; confirmed_flaky_threshold_met is the typed value when the policy threshold has been crossed; quarantined_flaky_under_active_quarantine pairs with a non-null quarantine_record_ref; cleared_after_stable_run_streak is the typed honest answer when a previously flaky test has accumulated a stable streak; history_insufficient_to_classify is the typed honest answer for too-few samples. flaky_classification_unknown_requires_review fails closed.

flaky_signal_class.description: Closed flaky-signal vocabulary. Names the typed reason a test was classified as flaky. ai_inferred_signal_provisional MUST cite an authoritative anchor ref under the truth-class rules (the typed flaky classification rides at least one observed signal in addition to the AI inference). flaky_signal_class_unknown_requires_review fails closed.

history_window_class.description: Closed history-window vocabulary. Names the typed sample window the flaky classification was computed over. last_n_attempts and last_n_days are the canonical fixed-size windows; last_main_branch_only narrows the sample to attempts on the main branch; last_session_only narrows to the current session (no cross-session aggregation); policy_defined_rolling_window cites an admin policy rule. history_window_unknown_requires_review fails closed.

quarantine_authority_class.description: Closed quarantine-authority vocabulary. Mirrors the cancellation_authority_class on schemas/execution/attempt.schema.json so a reviewer reads quarantine and cancellation authority with the same eyes. Every value outside not_quarantined and quarantine_authority_unknown_requires_review MUST cite a typed actor ref on the quarantine record.

quarantine_reason_class.description: Closed quarantine-reason vocabulary. Names the typed reason a test was quarantined. ai_assist_recommendation_pending_human_review is the typed honest answer for an AI-suggested quarantine that has not yet been confirmed by a human; the reason MUST NOT silently graduate to flaky_threshold_exceeded without a human review. quarantine_reason_unknown_requires_review fails closed.

quarantine_state_class.description: Closed quarantine-state vocabulary. active_quarantine is the typed value for a quarantine in force; expired_pending_re_evaluation is the typed honest mid-state when the typed expires_at has passed but no re-evaluation has run; lifted_* values name the typed lift path; superseded_by_newer_quarantine is the typed value when a newer quarantine record has replaced this one. quarantine_state_unknown_requires_review fails closed.

errored_count.description: Count of attempts in errored_outside_assertion (fixture / setup error).

stale_or_partial_count.description: Count of attempts whose outcome event was outcome_stale_output or outcome_partially_complete on the parent run (the test item's contribution to the partially-complete outcome).

total_attempt_count.description: Total attempts the tally aggregates over. MUST equal the sum of passed_count + failed_count + errored_count + timed_out_count + skipped_count + cancelled_count + stale_or_partial_count.

outcome_tally.description: Compact projection of per-state attempt counts for the flaky-history record. Counts mirror the outcome-event vocabulary on schemas/execution/run.schema.json so a reviewer reads the tally and the underlying outcome events with the same eyes.

canonical_test_item_id.description: Stable canonical id resolving to test_item_record.canonical_test_item_id on schemas/execution/test_discovery_state.schema.json. The history record cross-links by canonical id rather than by test_item_record_id so flake history persists across discovery refreshes.

history_window_size.description: Sample size for the window (e.g. 50 for last_50_attempts, 14 for last_14_days, etc.). Pinned so a reviewer can reproduce the threshold computation.

observed_signals.description: Ordered set of typed signals observed in the window. Required to be non-empty when flaky_classification_class is one of suspected_flaky_recent_intermittent, confirmed_flaky_threshold_met, or quarantined_flaky_under_active_quarantine. Empty for not_flaky_stable_history, cleared_after_stable_run_streak, history_insufficient_to_classify.

first_observed_intermittency_at.description: Optional timestamp of the first observed intermittency in the window. Null for not_flaky_stable_history and history_insufficient_to_classify.

last_observed_intermittency_at.description: Optional timestamp of the most recent intermittency in the window. Null for not_flaky_stable_history and history_insufficient_to_classify.

stable_streak_length.description: Optional count of consecutive stable (non-intermittent) attempts at the head of the window. Required when flaky_classification_class = cleared_after_stable_run_streak.

ai_inference_anchor_refs.description: Required when observed_signals contains ai_inferred_signal_provisional and at least one observed (non-AI) signal. The contract forbids a confirmed flake classification that rides only on AI inference.

quarantine_record_ref.description: Required when flaky_classification_class = quarantined_flaky_under_active_quarantine. Cites the quarantine_record on this schema.

outcome_event_refs.description: Optional ordered refs to the outcome_event_record entries (on schemas/execution/run.schema.json) included in the window. Empty when the history is reprojected from a tally without resolving each outcome.

allOf.description: Suspected / confirmed / quarantined classifications MUST cite at least one observed signal.
Quarantined-flaky classification MUST cite a quarantine_record_ref.
AI-inferred signals MUST be paired with an authoritative anchor ref or with at least one non-AI observed signal.
Cleared-after-stable-streak classification MUST cite the streak length.

flaky_history_record.description: One flaky-history record. Aggregates per-test-item attempt outcomes across a typed history window so a reviewer can answer 'is this test currently flaky, what signals justify the classification, what is the recent tally, and is it currently quarantined' without resolving every individual outcome event.

canonical_test_item_id.description: Stable canonical id resolving to test_item_record.canonical_test_item_id on schemas/execution/test_discovery_state.schema.json. Quarantine records cross-link by canonical id rather than by test_item_record_id so quarantine state persists across discovery refreshes.

flaky_history_record_ref.description: Optional ref to the flaky_history_record on this schema. Required when quarantine_reason_class = flaky_threshold_exceeded; null when the quarantine is not flake-driven (known-failing, environment-dependent, hardware-dependent, etc.).

quarantine_actor_ref.description: Required when quarantine_authority_class is anything other than not_quarantined or quarantine_authority_unknown_requires_review. Resolves to the typed actor (user identity for command-dispatch, admin policy epoch for policy pack, supervisor for collapse, threshold rule id for automated flake policy, session quarantine row for inheritance).

command_dispatch_descriptor_ref.description: Required when quarantine_authority_class = quarantine_by_user_via_command_dispatch. Null otherwise.

admin_policy_epoch_ref.description: Required when quarantine_authority_class = quarantine_by_admin_policy_pack. Null otherwise.

predecessor_quarantine_record_ref.description: Required when quarantine_state_class = superseded_by_newer_quarantine. Cites the quarantine record this one supersedes.

expires_at.description: Optional typed expiry deadline. Required when quarantine_state_class = active_quarantine; null after the quarantine has lifted, expired, or been superseded. Past the deadline, the typed state MUST narrow to expired_pending_re_evaluation rather than silently lifting.

lifted_at.description: Optional timestamp of the lift event. Required when quarantine_state_class is one of lifted_after_stable_streak, lifted_by_user_via_command_dispatch, or lifted_by_policy_change.

allOf.description: Authority outside not_quarantined / unknown MUST cite a quarantine_actor_ref.
User-initiated quarantine MUST cite a command_dispatch_descriptor_ref.
Policy-initiated quarantine MUST cite an admin_policy_epoch_ref.
Automated-flake quarantine MUST cite an automated_threshold_rule_ref.
Active quarantine MUST carry a typed expires_at deadline.
Lifted states MUST cite a lifted_at timestamp.
User-initiated lift MUST cite the lift command-dispatch descriptor.
Superseded state MUST cite the predecessor quarantine record.

quarantine_record.description: One quarantine record. Names the typed authority (user / admin policy / supervisor / automated threshold / session inheritance), the typed reason (flaky / known-failing / environment-dependent / etc.), the typed state (active / expired / lifted / superseded), and the typed expiry / lift / supersede refs so a reviewer can audit a quarantine without opening raw bodies. The contract forbids silent lift after expiry: an expired quarantine MUST narrow to expired_pending_re_evaluation rather than silently transitioning to lifted_*.
